strategy_summary: The mouth of bloody-nose beetles deters predators via noxious liquid secretions. “Other animals that autohemorrhage come from various insect families within the beetle order Coleoptera.
mechanism:
The mouth of bloody-nose beetles deters predators via noxious liquid secretions.

“Other animals that autohemorrhage come from various insect families within the beetle order Coleoptera. One such creature is the bloody-nosed beetle ( Timarcha spp.), which forces bright-red blood out around its mouth when threatened.” (Shuker 2001:130)
